judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on a technical map/diagram with an oval-shaped impact pattern, a dense cluster, and a single large dot, but they disagree on the geographic location and disagree on quoted text, indicating inconsistency on visual details.
original_image_tags: A black-and-white technical map showing the southeastern United States coastline. An oval-shaped impact dispersion pattern, composed of numerous small dots, is centered over land, likely in the region of South Carolina and North Carolina. A denser, darker concentration of dots marks the center of the oval. A single, larger black dot is located within the oval, slightly southwest of the center. The coastline is rendered with a simple outline, showing Florida, Georgia, South Carolina, and North Carolina.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A technical diagram labeled “Figure 7. Atlas IIAS Impacts with No Breakup,” showing a large dotted circular impact footprint over an outlined map-like coastline, with vertical text reading “Atlas IIAS Impacts Random-Attitude Failures at 130 sec. Thrust to 280 sec. No Breakup.”*
